title: achillea ptarmica subsp. ptarmicifolia
intro: Achillea ptarmica subsp. ptarmicifolia, also known as Sneezewort or False Yarrow, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Sneezewort include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve headaches and joint pain, and act as a natural expectorant to help clear congestion. Herbal preparations of Sneezewort can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
